function.name: start_irrigation_zone
function.description: Begin watering an irrigation zone for a given duration.

prompt: Water the rose bed for 15 minutes.
Start watering the front lawn for 20 minutes.
Stop watering the back lawn.
Turn on the patio lights.
Turn off the driveway lights.
Set the pool heater to 28 degrees.
Turn the pool pump on.
Close the pool cover.
Open the pool cover please.

function.name: query_soil_moisture
function.description: Read the soil moisture sensor of a zone.

prompt: What's the soil moisture in the vegetable garden?
What's the pool temperature right now?
Make the patio lights blue.

function.name: set_garden_lawnmower
function.description: Start or stop the robot lawnmower.

prompt: Start the lawnmower.
Stop the lawnmower.
Turn on the garden speaker.
Water the citrus trees for 25 minutes.
Water the hedge row for 10 minutes.
Run the driveway strip irrigation for 5 minutes.
Water the succulent corner for 3 minutes.
Cancel the rose bed watering.
Stop watering the vegetable garden.
Switch on the garden path lights.
Turn the backyard floodlights on.
Kill the porch lights.
Turn off the deck lights.
Set the deck lights to warm white.
Turn the porch lights red.
Set the garden path lights to green.
Heat the pool to 30 degrees.
Set pool target to 26 C.
Turn off the pool pump.
Run the pool pump on high.
Cover the pool.
Uncover the pool.
Check the soil moisture for the rose bed.
How dry is the front lawn?
Read the moisture sensor on the citrus trees.
How warm is the pool?
Mow the back lawn.
Send the lawnmower out to the front lawn.
Switch off the patio speaker.
Play the outdoor speaker at volume 6.
Water the back lawn for half an hour.
Water the front lawn for one hour.
Run the rose bed sprinklers for 12 min.
Sprinkle the vegetable garden for 8 minutes.
Turn on the pool deck lights.
Turn off the pool deck lights.
Make the backyard lights purple.
Set pool heater to 29.
Crank the pool heater to 31.
Run the pool pump on low.
Pull the pool cover closed.
Roll back the pool cover.
Check the hedge row soil moisture.
What's the moisture level in the succulent corner?
Mow the front lawn now.
Set garden speaker volume to 3.
Turn off the patio speaker.
Water citrus trees for 18 minutes.
Run hedge row irrigation for 7 min.
Halt the citrus trees watering.
Stop the succulent corner irrigation.
Flip on the gazebo lights.
Switch off the gazebo lights.
Turn driveway lights yellow.
Make pool deck lights cyan.
Tell me the current pool water temperature.
Park the lawnmower.
Crank the pool deck speaker to volume 8.

function.name: schedule_irrigation
function.description: Schedule a future irrigation event for a zone.

prompt: Schedule the rose bed to water tomorrow at 6am for 20 minutes.
Set a schedule for the front lawn every morning at 7am for 25 minutes.
Water the citrus trees every other day at 5:30am for 15 minutes.
Schedule vegetable garden irrigation for tonight at 9pm, 10 minutes.
Add the hedge row to the Saturday morning 8am watering for 12 min.
It's been hot today, do the usual morning watering on the back lawn but for 35 minutes.
The veggies look thirsty, give them an extra 15 min.
It didn't rain last night, water the roses for 20 minutes.
Do a quick top-up on the front lawn, 6 minutes should do.
Light up the back patio, we're having guests.
Make the patio lights a soft amber.
Set the garden lights to ocean blue for the party.
Warm up the pool to 27.5 for tomorrow's swim.
Turn off the pool heater for the season.
Switch the pool pump to medium speed.
It's getting cold tonight, put the pool cover on.
Take the pool cover off, we're swimming.
Before I water it, check the moisture on the vegetable garden first.
Is the pool warm enough for the kids?
Send the lawnmower out to do the back lawn.
Turn the garden speaker on and set it to volume 4.
Lower the outdoor speaker to volume 2.
Hit the back lawn with the usual morning watering.
Do a routine morning water on the hedge row.
It's a scorcher, double the rose bed water to 30 min.
Schedule the driveway strip for Sunday 7am, 5 minutes.
Add succulent corner to Monday 6am schedule for 4 min.
Light up the path to the pool, please.
It's getting late, kill the garden path lights.
For the dinner party, make the patio lights a romantic pink.
Get the pool to a comfortable 28.5 by morning.
Crank the pump to high speed for filtering.
Pool cover on, the leaves are everywhere.
Open the cover, I want to take a dip.
How dry are the citrus trees today?
Have the lawnmower do the front lawn this afternoon.
Bump the outdoor speaker up to volume 7 for the bbq.
Looks dry, run the citrus trees for 22 minutes.
Looks soaked enough, kill the rose bed water now.
Schedule rose bed watering for every Wednesday at 6:15am for 18 minutes.
Plan back lawn irrigation for Friday at 5:45am, 25 min.
Turn on the side yard floodlight, I heard a noise.
All clear, you can shut off the side yard light.
For Halloween, make the porch lights orange.
Turn the backyard lights to a moonlight white for movie night.
My friends want a hot swim, bring the pool to 32 degrees.
Drop the pump down to low for the night.
We're leaving for the weekend, close the pool cover.
Give me the pool temp reading.
Robomower to the hedge row area, please.
Quiet please, drop the patio speaker to 1.
Heat wave today, give the back lawn 45 minutes.
Wow it's pouring out, kill the front lawn sprinklers.
Book the vegetable garden for Sunday 6:30am for 16 min.
Christmas vibe, set the front yard lights to red and green - red for now.
Bring pool down to a cool 24 - it's been too warm.
Mower seems stuck, please stop it.
Read me the moisture on the driveway strip area.
Plan an evening soak for the rose bed at 8pm for 14 min.
Top up the vegetable garden with another 9 min of water.
If the rose bed soil is below 30% moisture I want it watered for 18 minutes, but for now just kick off that watering.
Forecast says no rain for three days - set a recurring schedule on the citrus trees daily at 5am for 20 min starting tomorrow.
Pool party Saturday afternoon - first thing, get the heater on at 29.5.
Storm rolling in - shut everything down for the pool, start with closing the cover.
Romantic evening on the patio - dim the patio lights to a deep red, that's the priority.
Yesterday I skipped watering because of clouds but it never rained, give the front lawn 28 minutes right now to make up.
Before I commit to extra watering this week, get me the current moisture on the back lawn first.
Plan a deep pre-dawn soak for the vegetable garden - 4:30am on weekdays only, 22 minutes each time.
Doing chemical balance test today - I need the pump running on high for the next hour.
Setting the mood for the pool party - first job, turn the pool deck lights into a tropical aqua green.
Lawn looks shaggy and it hasn't rained in a week - get the mower onto the back lawn now.
Late night chill session - drop the patio speaker to volume 2 first, we'll do lights after.
Kids might want to swim later - tell me the current pool temperature so I can decide whether to heat it.
My in-laws are coming and the hedge row looks brown - hit it with 26 min of water immediately.
Winterizing the pool - kill the heater entirely.
We're going on vacation Mon-Fri next week - set up the front lawn irrigation for every day at 6:00am for 22 min while we're gone.
It's pitch black out there and I need to find my keys - kick on the driveway lights asap.
Oh no, the rain just started but the sprinklers are still running on the citrus trees - turn them off now.
After the mower finishes the front lawn at noon, I want it then doing the back lawn too - send it to the back lawn now since front is done.
Outdoor cinema night for the kids - first thing, make the backyard lights a deep navy.
